examples.title: Pseudocode: Serverless Function Execution Flow
examples.description: Demonstrates the event-driven execution model of serverless computing (FaaS) where functions are triggered by events without managing servers
examples.code:
ALGORITHM ServerlessFunctionExecution
INPUT: Event Data (from API Gateway, S3, Queue, etc.)
OUTPUT: Response from Function Execution

BEGIN
  // Step 1: Event Trigger Detection
  FUNCTION ReceiveEvent(eventSource, eventData)
    PRINT "Event received from: " + eventSource
    RETURN eventData
  END FUNCTION

  // Step 2: Function Runtime Initialization
  FUNCTION InitializeRuntime(functionConfig)
    IF coldStartRequired THEN
      PRINT "Initializing runtime environment"
      loadDependencies()
      initializeConnection()
    ELSE
      PRINT "Using warm execution environment"
    END IF
    RETURN runtimeContext
  END FUNCTION

  // Step 3: Execute Business Logic
  FUNCTION ExecuteFunction(eventData, runtimeContext)
    PRINT "Executing function logic"
    
    // Parse event data
    parsedEvent = parseEvent(eventData)
    
    // Execute application logic
    result = processEvent(parsedEvent)
    
    // Handle external services if needed
    IF result.requiresDatabase THEN
      dbResult = queryDatabase(result.query)
      result = enrichResult(result, dbResult)
    END IF
    
    RETURN result
  END FUNCTION

  // Step 4: Scale Based on Load
  FUNCTION AutoScale(currentInvocations, threshold)
    IF currentInvocations > threshold THEN
      PRINT "Scaling out: Adding new function instances"
      provisionNewInstance()
    ELSE IF currentInvocations < threshold/2 THEN
      PRINT "Scaling in: Reducing function instances"
      terminateInstance()
    END IF
  END FUNCTION

  // Step 5: Return Response
  FUNCTION ReturnResponse(executionResult)
    RETURN {
      statusCode: 200,
      body: executionResult,
      headers: {"Content-Type": "application/json"}
    }
  END FUNCTION

  // Main Execution Flow
  eventData = ReceiveEvent(TRIGGER_SOURCE, INPUT_EVENT)
  runtimeCtx = InitializeRuntime(FUNCTION_CONFIG)
  result = ExecuteFunction(eventData, runtimeCtx)
  AutoScale(currentLoad, SCALE_THRESHOLD)
  response = ReturnResponse(result)
  
  PRINT "Function execution completed"
  RETURN response
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode illustrates the serverless computing execution model: 1) Events trigger function execution from various sources (API Gateway, S3, queues), 2) Runtime initializes (cold start loads dependencies, warm reuse), 3) Business logic executes with optional external service calls, 4) Auto-scaling adjusts based on load, 5) Response returns to caller. Key characteristics: pay-per-use billing, stateless design, and automatic scaling.

examples.title: Python: AWS Lambda Event Processor
examples.description: Implements a serverless Lambda function that processes S3 upload events, demonstrating event-driven serverless computing
examples.explanation: This Python implementation demonstrates AWS Lambda serverless computing: 1) Event-driven triggers from S3, 2) Connection reuse with boto3 clients initialized outside handler, 3) Event parsing and conditional logic based on file types, 4) Integration with DynamoDB for metadata storage, 5) Proper error handling and HTTP response format. Key serverless concepts: automatic scaling, pay-per-use (only runs when triggered), cold/warm starts. Time Complexity: O(n) where n is number of records, Space Complexity: O(1) excluding external service storage.

examples.title: Python: Dockerfile for Container-Native Development
examples.description: Demonstrates container-native development with a multi-stage Dockerfile for a Python web application, showcasing modern DevOps practices
examples.code:
# Multi-stage Dockerfile for production-ready Python application
# Build Stage: Install dependencies and build application
FROM python:3.11-slim AS builder

WORKDIR /build

# Install build dependencies
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y \
    gcc \
    libc-dev \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Create virtual environment
RUN python -m venv /opt/venv
ENV PATH="/opt/venv/bin:$PATH"

# Install Python dependencies
COPY requirements.txt .
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir --upgrade pip && \
    pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt

# Production Stage: Minimal runtime image
FROM python:3.11-slim AS production

# Create non-root user for security
RUN groupadd -r appgroup && useradd -r -g appgroup appuser

WORKDIR /app

# Copy virtual environment from builder
COPY --from=builder /opt/venv /opt/venv
ENV PATH="/opt/venv/bin:$PATH"

# Copy application code
COPY --chown=appuser:appgroup ./src ./src
COPY --chown=appuser:appgroup ./config ./config

# Expose application port
EXPOSE 8000

# Set environment variables
ENV PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 \
    PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1 \
    ENVIRONMENT=production

# Switch to non-root user
USER appuser

# Health check
HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=10s --start-period=5s --retries=3 \
    CMD python -c "import urllib.request; urllib.request.urlopen('http://localhost:8000/health')" || exit 1

# Run application
CMD ["python", "-m", "uvicorn", "src.main:app", "--host", "0.0.0.0", "--port", "8000"]
examples.explanation: This Dockerfile demonstrates container-native development best practices: 1) Multi-stage builds reduce final image size, 2) Non-root user for security (Principle of Least Privilege), 3) Virtual environment isolation, 4) Health checks for container orchestration readiness, 5) Environment variables for configuration, 6) Layer caching optimization. This is used with Kubernetes/ECS for container orchestration. The EXPOSE and CMD instructions configure how the containerized application runs in production environments.

examples.title: Python: Kubernetes Deployment Manifest
examples.description: Demonstrates container orchestration with Kubernetes, deploying a scalable containerized application with auto-scaling and health checks
examples.code:
---
# Kubernetes Deployment for Container Orchestration
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: cloud-app-deployment
  labels:
    app: cloud-app
    version: v1
spec:
  # Replica set configuration
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: cloud-app
  
  # Rolling update strategy
  strategy:
    type: RollingUpdate
    rollingUpdate:
      maxSurge: 1
      maxUnavailable: 0
  
  # Pod template specification
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: cloud-app
        version: v1
    spec:
      # Container configuration
      containers:
      - name: cloud-app
        image: myregistry/cloud-app:1.0.0
        imagePullPolicy: Always
        
        ports:
        - containerPort: 8000
          name: http
          protocol: TCP
        
        # Resource limits and requests
        resources:
          requests:
            memory: "256Mi"
            cpu: "250m"
          limits:
            memory: "512Mi"
            cpu: "500m"
        
        # Environment variables
        env:
        - name: DATABASE_URL
          valueFrom:
            secretKeyRef:
              name: app-secrets
              key: database-url
        - name: ENVIRONMENT
          value: "production"
        
        # Health checks
        livenessProbe:
          httpGet:
            path: /health
            port: 8000
          initialDelaySeconds: 30
          periodSeconds: 10
          timeoutSeconds: 5
          failureThreshold: 3
        
        readinessProbe:
          httpGet:
            path: /ready
            port: 8000
          initialDelaySeconds: 5
          periodSeconds: 5
          timeoutSeconds: 3
          failureThreshold: 3
        
        # Volume mounts
        volumeMounts:
        - name: app-config
          mountPath: /app/config
          readOnly: true
      
      # Security context
      securityContext:
        runAsNonRoot: true
        runAsUser: 1000
        fsGroup: 1000
      
      # Volumes
      volumes:
      - name: app-config
        configMap:
          name: app-config
---
# Kubernetes Service for load balancing
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: cloud-app-service
  labels:
    app: cloud-app
spec:
  type: LoadBalancer
  selector:
    app: cloud-app
  ports:
  - name: http
    port: 80
    targetPort: 8000
    protocol: TCP
---
# Horizontal Pod Autoscaler
apiVersion: autoscaling/v2
kind: HorizontalPodAutoscaler
metadata:
  name: cloud-app-hpa
spec:
  scaleTargetRef:
    apiVersion: apps/v1
    kind: Deployment
    name: cloud-app-deployment
  minReplicas: 3
  maxReplicas: 10
  metrics:
  - type: Resource
    resource:
      name: cpu
      target:
        type: Utilization
        averageUtilization: 70
  - type: Resource
    resource:
      name: memory
      target:
        type: Utilization
        averageUtilization: 80
examples.explanation: This Kubernetes manifest demonstrates container orchestration: 1) Deployment manages replica sets with rolling updates, 2) Service provides load balancing across pods, 3) HorizontalPodAutoscaler enables auto-scaling based on CPU/memory metrics, 4) Resource limits ensure fair scheduling, 5) Liveness/readiness probes for health monitoring, 6) Security context for container isolation, 7) Secrets and ConfigMaps for configuration management. This represents the core of modern cloud-native development on platforms like EKS, AKS, and GKE. Auto-scaling handles variable workloads efficiently.
